name: Procedural
description: Programming by specifying a sequence of structured steps and commands.

name: Object-Oriented
description: Organizing code into objects that encapsulate data and behavior.

name: Functional
description: Treating computation as the evaluation of mathematical functions, avoiding side effects.

name: Logic
description: Programming based on formal logic; specifying what should be achieved rather than how.

name: Array/Vector
description: Treating data as large arrays or vectors, performing operations on them collectively.

name: Concatenative
description: Programming based on the composition of functions, often using a stack-oriented approach.

name: Data-driven
description: Controlling the flow of a program based on the data itself, often through patterns or tables.

name: Actor-model
description: Using independent 'actors' that communicate via message passing to handle concurrency.

name: Reactive
description: Programming based on asynchronous data streams and the propagation of change.

name: Aspect-Oriented
description: Separating cross-cutting concerns (e.g., logging) from the main business logic.

name: Event-Driven
description: Flow determined by events such as user actions, sensor outputs, or message passing.

name: Generic
description: Writing code that can work with different data types while maintaining type safety.

name: Constraint
description: Solving problems by defining relationships (constraints) between variables.

name: Metaprogramming
description: Writing programs that treat other programs as their data; code that generates or modifies code.
